verses.text: If our earthly house dissolve, 'we have a building of God... eternal in the heavens.' Sure home. Death is but exchanging a tent for a mansion.
If our earthly house dissolve, 'we have a building of God... eternal in the heavens.' Sure home. Death is but exchanging a tent for a mansion.
We groan, longing for our heavenly house; God has given 'the earnest of the Spirit.' Pledge given. The Spirit assures us of the glory to come.
We groan, longing for our heavenly house; God has given 'the earnest of the Spirit.' Pledge given. The Spirit assures us of the glory to come.
We groan, longing for our heavenly house; God has given 'the earnest of the Spirit.' Pledge given. The Spirit assures us of the glory to come.
We are confident, 'willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.' Better with Christ. To depart is to be with Him.
We are confident, 'willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.' Better with Christ. To depart is to be with Him.
We are confident, 'willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.' Better with Christ. To depart is to be with Him.
We labour to be accepted; 'we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ.' Coming reckoning. All shall answer for the deeds done in the body.
We labour to be accepted; 'we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ.' Coming reckoning. All shall answer for the deeds done in the body.
Knowing 'the terror of the Lord, we persuade men'; we are manifest to God. Earnest pleading. The judgment to come stirs us to win souls.
Knowing 'the terror of the Lord, we persuade men'; we are manifest to God. Earnest pleading. The judgment to come stirs us to win souls.
Knowing 'the terror of the Lord, we persuade men'; we are manifest to God. Earnest pleading. The judgment to come stirs us to win souls.
'The love of Christ constraineth us'; He died for all, that they might live to Him. Compelling love. His love drives us to live for Him.
'The love of Christ constraineth us'; He died for all, that they might live to Him. Compelling love. His love drives us to live for Him.
'If any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; ... all things are become new.' New creation. Faith makes a man wholly new.
'If any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; ... all things are become new.' New creation. Faith makes a man wholly new.
God 'hath reconciled us to himself,' and given 'the ministry of reconciliation.' Reconciled. He restores us and sends us to call others.
God 'hath reconciled us to himself,' and given 'the ministry of reconciliation.' Reconciled. He restores us and sends us to call others.

We are 'ambassadors for Christ'; 'be ye reconciled'; He 'made him to be sin for us... that we might be made the righteousness of God.' Great exchange. Christ bore our sin to clothe us in His righteousness.
